Sometimes (specially in monorepos) it is possible that Tailwind CSS automatic class name detection finds some unwanted strings in your project and generate styles for them. To avoid this, you can disable automatic detection and register your source files using `@source` directive.

AI-generated sites arrive with outdated: AI-generated sites arrive with outdated dependencies, stale syntax, copy-paste layouts, the same color palettes, purple gradients, inconsistent spacing, and a structure so mediocre that no one wants to use it.

AI slop content and lack of structure: AI slop content and lack of structure
TRYING TO FIX THE SLOP WITH INSTRUCTIONS: TRYING TO FIX THE SLOP WITH INSTRUCTIONS?
Your LLM ignores the instructions and sk: Your LLM ignores the instructions and skills.
Agent skills are collections of instruct: Agent skills are collections of instructions and rules. Check the logs and the pattern is familiar: the model reads a few files, skips the rest, ignores inconvenient rules, and moves on.
UI work needs more context than most tas: UI work needs more context than most tasks. Yet as the instruction set grows, the chance of selective reading grows with it.
Ignored the rules and treated them as su: Ignored the rules and treated them as suggestions only
The model continued without following th: The model continued without following the rules it was expected to follow.
Wasted time, wasted effort, wasted token: Wasted time, wasted effort, wasted tokens
Without clear instructions, AI creates t: Without clear instructions, AI creates the same generic UI for everyone. That's why trends like purple buttons and gradients quickly became overused.
Making the design unique takes many prom: Making the design unique takes many prompts, additional edits, wasted time, and LOTS of tokens.
you could one-shot a unique, original, a: you could one-shot a unique, original, and functional UI without any effort?
No slop. No skip. No waste.
Every tool has a defined job and a stric: Every tool has a defined job and a strict execution order. Each tool provides the necessary resources and functionality to your agent. The agent must follow these rules sequentially. It cannot skip steps, invent component syntax, or mark unfinished work as complete.
LLM is forced to read and follow: LLM is forced to read and follow
Blueprint gives your agent the tools to: Blueprint gives your agent the tools to understand the source first. It extracts the structure, chooses the matching daisyUI components, supplies exact snippets, and checks the conversion when the code is done.
Picture color palette to a daisyUI theme: Picture color palette to a daisyUI theme
Watch Blueprint's MCP server create uniq: Watch Blueprint's MCP server create unique, authentic UIs from a simple prompt. The server gives your AI agent the context it needs: design-system principles, code snippets, and UX direction. When the page is finished, Blueprint inspects the code and fixes visual issues.
Blueprint MCP server converts a Figma de: Blueprint MCP server converts a Figma design to daisyUI
Blueprint MCP server converts Tailwind C: Blueprint MCP server converts Tailwind CSS to daisyUI

The Tailwind CSS, Bootstrap, and Screens: The Tailwind CSS, Bootstrap, and Screenshot to daisyUI prompts, plus Image to daisyUI theme, are now available as MCP tools. MCP clients that don't support MCP prompts can use them too.
Reads the repo first. It understands dep: Reads the repo first. It understands dependencies, framework, configuration, themes, colors, fonts, icons, and file structure before your agent changes a pixel.

Loads implementation, syntax, accessibil: Loads implementation, syntax, accessibility, responsive, theme, media, and quality rules as requirements your LLM must follow.
The workflow cannot continue by skimming: The workflow cannot continue by skimming half a skill file and quietly dropping the difficult constraints.
Enforced design rules and code instructi: Enforced design rules and code instructions
Directs UX, content, originality, color,: Directs UX, content, originality, color, composition, typography, imagery, design trends, and motion as one coherent idea.
Instead of another purple-gradient templ: Instead of another purple-gradient template, the page gets a point of view connected to its product and audience.
Chooses from 211 page architectures cove: Chooses from 211 page architectures covering purpose, structure, content, goals, states, edge cases, and component composition.
Your LLM stops guessing what belongs on: Your LLM stops guessing what belongs on the page. It gets a production plan shaped around the job the page must do.
Retrieves exact, correct daisyUI compone: Retrieves exact, correct daisyUI component structures, variants, code snippets, and examples only when the page needs them.
No stale syntax from model memory. No ut: No stale syntax from model memory. No utility pile pretending to be a maintained component.
Audits the finished source for visual pr: Audits the finished source for visual problems, syntax errors, accessibility issues, responsive failures, and ignored rules.
When the inspection finds a problem, you: When the inspection finds a problem, your agent has to fix it before the workflow can call the page finished.

Is this a one-time payment or a subscrip: Is this a one-time payment or a subscription?
Both are possible. You can purchase a mo: Both are possible. You can purchase a monthly license, yearly license, or lifetime license.
You will receive an email with a license: You will receive an email with a license key that you can use to activate the MCP server. If you don't see the email, check your spam folder.
Yes. Blueprint is updated automatically: Yes. Blueprint is updated automatically with the latest version of daisyUI. New MCP tools, resources, and prompts are added automatically.
We offer support via Discord. Join the d: We offer support via Discord. Join the daisyUI Discord and ask your questions there.
Yes. You can cancel at any time from the: Yes. You can cancel at any time from the order portal. You will keep access until the end of your billing cycle.
Digital products are not refundable. If: Digital products are not refundable. If there was a payment mistake, such as a duplicate payment, email us and we will help resolve it.
Check your spam folder first. If you sti: Check your spam folder first. If you still can't find it, email help@daisyui.com.
Email help@daisyui.com and we will help: Email help@daisyui.com and we will help you get the right package.
If the payment failed and you did not re: If the payment failed and you did not receive the product, try again. If money was deducted, it is usually refunded automatically within one or two weeks. Email help@daisyui.com if the issue persists.
Install Grok Build if you haven't alread: Install Grok Build if you haven't already.
Run this command to add the Blueprint MC: Run this command to add the Blueprint MCP server. The `FIGMA` API key is optional and only needed for Figma-to-code conversion.
Check the connection with `grok mcp doct: Check the connection with `grok mcp doctor daisyui-blueprint`. Read the Grok Build MCP docs for project-scoped configuration and troubleshooting.
LLMs often produce generic AI slop by de: LLMs often produce generic AI slop by default. They can skim skill files, ignore difficult instructions, hallucinate component syntax, and settle for mediocre design. You then spend more time, effort, and tokens asking for edits.
Blueprint solves this with a guided MCP: Blueprint solves this with a guided MCP workflow. Six tools walk the LLM through the task, supply rules and resources one step at a time, and confirm the LLM understood them. The LLM then writes the code. A final inspection finds quality problems for the LLM to fix.

Provides current daisyUI resources for e: Provides current daisyUI resources for each required component:
This lets the LLM use maintained daisyUI: This lets the LLM use maintained daisyUI syntax instead of recalling stale or invented classes from training data.
Visual hierarchy, spacing, density, and: Visual hierarchy, spacing, density, and composition
